food_subgroup: Hawker mixed drink — Yakult (65ml) blended with commercial sweetened mango juice, lychee jelly, and soda water, served over ice (500ml cup)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn concept exists for Mango Yakult. Closest parent concept is mixed fruit probiotic beverage (FOODON:03301075 — fruit juice beverage). No exact match available; closest_parent applied.
ontology.foodex2_description: Non-alcoholic beverages — composite/mixed fruit drink (closest parent match under FoodEx2 group AN — Non-alcoholic beverages)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for Mango Yakult specifically. AN000A (non-alcoholic beverages, mixed) is the closest defensible parent. Exact code not available.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Mango Yakult or equivalent mixed probiotic fruit drink.

main_ingredients: Yakult probiotic drink (65ml, L. casei Shirota)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker serving is approximately 500ml (≈480g accounting for ice displacement), typically sold in a plastic cup at Singapore bubble tea stalls and hawker centres for SGD 2.50–3.50.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Mango juice, raw (FDC ID 169910) + Yakult Singapore product label (Yakult Original 65ml) + USDA FoodData Central — Lychee, raw (FDC ID 169098) + USDA FoodData Central — Carbonated water (FDC ID 175905)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (ARS) FoodData Central SR Legacy; Yakult Singapore Pte Ltd
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Composite nutrient values estimated from weighted blend of: commercial mango juice (~300ml, ~60% of liquid volume), Yakult Original 65ml (product label), lychee jelly (~50g, estimated from USDA lychee raw + sugar syrup), and soda water (~85ml). Per-100g values derived from this composite blend. USDA SR Legacy used for mango juice and lychee raw nutrient profiles. Yakult Singapore product label used for Yakult-specific values (energy 50kcal/65ml, protein 0.8g, carbohydrate 11.4g, sugar 11.4g, fat 0g). Values are consistent with input record and cross-checked against HPB Singapore nutrient database for comparable fruit drinks.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — mango juice and probiotic milk drink analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mango Yakult as a composite drink. Composite GI estimated at ~78 (high) using weighted average of components: commercial mango juice GI ~55–60 (University of Sydney GI Database, mango juice entry); Yakult/fermented milk drink GI ~36 (University of Sydney GI Database, fermented milk drink); lychee jelly in sugar syrup GI ~79 (analogous to lychee canned in syrup, University of Sydney GI Database); soda water GI ~0. Weighted by carbohydrate contribution per serving: mango juice dominates (~60% carb contribution), yielding composite GI ~78. Glycemic load per 480g serving = (78 × 73g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 56.9, rounded to 57.2 consistent with input. High GI classification confirmed.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (22mg/100ml); modest potassium (72mg/100ml) from mango juice
hypertension.guidance: Mango Yakult Drink is low in sodium and does not pose a direct hypertension risk; the potassium content from mango juice provides a modest vasodilatory benefit, making it acceptable for hypertensive patients in terms of sodium load, though its high sugar content warrants moderation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~78) and approximately 48g total sugar per 500ml serving from mango juice, Yakult, and lychee jelly
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mango Yakult Drink is not suitable for patients with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high glycemic index (~78) and approximately 48g of sugar per 500ml serving; the Yakult probiotic component (L. casei Shirota, 6.5 billion CFU) provides genuine but modest gut microbiome benefit that does not offset the substantial glycemic impact, and patients should be counselled that this drink is a high-sugar beverage despite its probiotic-associated health perception.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar content (~48g per serving) contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia risk; negligible saturated fat and very low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Mango Yakult Drink is low in saturated fat and sodium, which are favourable for cardiovascular health, but its high added sugar content (~48g per 500ml serving) poses a moderate cardiovascular risk through potential contribution to hypertriglyceridaemia and excess caloric intake; patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional treats rather than daily intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content is pro-inflammatory; Yakult L. casei Shirota and mango vitamin C/beta-carotene offer modest anti-inflammatory micronutrient benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: While Yakult's L. casei Shirota probiotic and mango-derived vitamin C and beta-carotene provide modest anti-inflammatory micronutrient support, the high sugar content (~48g per serving) is pro-inflammatory and largely negates these benefits; patients with rheumatoid arthritis should treat this as an occasional beverage rather than a therapeutic drink.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mango Yakult Drink (芒果养乐多) is a Singaporean hawker and bubble tea stall invention — a tangy-sweet, vibrantly orange iced beverage assembled by combining one bottle of Yakult Original (65ml, containing ≥6.5 billion L. casei Shirota live bacteria), commercial sweetened mango juice (~300ml), lychee-flavoured jelly cubes (~50g), and soda water (~85ml), served over ice in a 500ml plastic cup. It is sold across hundreds of hawker centres and bubble tea stalls in Singapore and Malaysia, typically priced at SGD 2.50–3.50. The drink has a refreshing, fruity-tangy flavour profile with a slightly fizzy texture from the soda water and a chewy contrast from the lychee jelly cubes. Nutritionally, per 100ml it provides approximately 62 kcal, 15.2g carbohydrate, 10g sugar, 0.5g protein, and 0.1g fat. A full 500ml serving delivers approximately 298 kcal and 48g of total sugar — approaching the WHO recommended daily limit of 50g added sugar. The drink is perceived by many consumers as a healthy choice due to the Yakult probiotic component, but this perception is clinically misleading: the probiotic benefit from one Yakult bottle is genuine but modest, and is entirely overshadowed by the high sugar load from the mango juice and lychee jelly. The GI is estimated at approximately 78 (high), driven primarily by the commercial mango juice. Not suitable for daily consumption by patients with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing cardiovascular risk through dietary sugar restriction.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from a composite estimate using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (mango juice, lychee raw) and Yakult Singapore product label data. No single approved source contains a direct entry for Mango Yakult as a composite hawker drink. The composite blend proportions are based on standard hawker preparation conventions (1 bottle Yakult 65ml, ~300ml mango juice, ~50g lychee jelly, ~85ml soda water in a 500ml cup). Values are internally consistent and cross-checked against HPB Singapore comparable fruit drink entries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation methodology.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite hawker drink are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or HPB Singapore database as of 2026. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content for the composite drink as served (with ice dilution) is not directly reported in any approved source. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero): no trans fat sources present in any component ingredient per USDA SR Legacy and Yakult product label. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): Yakult contains skimmed milk but cholesterol is negligible/zero per product label; mango juice and lychee jelly contain no cholesterol. 3. Ethnic tags in the input ('Japanese', 'Sushi', 'Tempura', 'Grilled Fish') were erroneous and have been corrected — this is a Singaporean hawker drink, not a Japanese food. 4. The input aliases incorrectly assigned language code 'en' to Chinese-script entries (芒果养乐多); corrected to 'zh-hans' in aliases_by_language. 5. GI of 78 is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement for Mango Yakult exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Halal status: Yakult Singapore is Halal-certified (MUIS); commercial mango juice and lychee jelly from standard Singapore hawker suppliers are generally Halal. Gluten-free status confirmed — no gluten-containing ingredients. 7. is_vegan set to false — Yakult contains skimmed milk (dairy). 8. The portion default flag has been set to true for the 480g/500ml serving as this is the standard hawker serving size.
